classification_rationale: Vendor uses AI for operational decisioning on KYC and loan-document intake, processing high-sensitivity PII (SIN, government IDs, financial records) through both proprietary vision models and a third-party fallback (OpenAI). Cross-border data flow (CA customer data routed to US OpenAI on low-confidence pages) plus the absence of a current ISO 27001 attestation together raise the tier from operational Tier 2 to elevated Tier 3. Senior review is recommended over conditional approval because the OpenAI fallback path is the most material risk and warrants explicit decisioning by an accountable owner rather than a delegated approval.

evidence_cited.reasoning: Reported as 'operational_decisions'. Combined with the high-sensitivity PII categories disclosed below, this sits at the Tier 3 boundary rather than Tier 2.

Categories include SIN and government ID numbers (high-sensitivity per docs/phase-1 PII taxonomy). For a Canadian deploying organization, SIN handling brings PIPEDA and OSFI E-23 sensitivity expectations into scope.

Identity document OCR feeds the KYC flow. KYC is a regulated decision under both AML guidance and OSFI E-23 onboarding controls; the AI is in the operational pre-decision step but materially shapes the human decision.

OpenAI listed as fallback for low-confidence pages. This is a cross-border AI sub-processor receiving PII (redacted on first-pass detection, but vision OCR cannot guarantee complete pre-redaction). The fallback path is the most material risk in the submission.
Canadian customer data pinned to ca-central-1 in vendor's primary path, but OpenAI fallback calls go to US. The conditional-residency arrangement requires explicit consent capture and audit trail.
SOC 2 Type II is current (2025-09) but no ISO 27001 listed. For a vendor in the KYC pre-decisioning path, ISO 27001 is the expected control attestation; absence is a gap, not a blocker.
OpenAI Inc. disclosed as US-based subprocessor processing customer data. Disclosure is correct; the underlying flow is the elevated-risk path.

required_mitigations: Obtain explicit DPA addendum from TextLens covering OpenAI fallback path: data minimization, no-retention guarantee, and PII-redaction verification before contract execution.
Require ISO 27001 certification (or equivalent control attestation) within 12 months as a condition of continued use.
Configure enterprise-tier ca-central-1 pinning for primary OCR path; obtain written confirmation that the configuration is applied.
Implement runtime monitoring on the OpenAI fallback trigger rate; investigate if fallback exceeds 5% of documents (indicates pre-redaction is ineffective).
Quarterly review of customer-consented production samples used for model improvement (the opt-in mechanism) to confirm opt-out rate and audit consent records.
accountable_owner: Chief Risk Officer or designated Senior Vendor Risk Manager
